context: Tests whether the agent implements persistence on the producer side (between detector and actuator), tunes persistence duration to ~0.8s, and correctly understands that producer-side persistence and actuator-side debounce are separate complementary layers.

checklist.name: miss_streak counter
checklist.description: presence_buffer.py tracks a consecutive-miss counter (miss streak) that increments on each no-detection frame and resets on detection

checklist.name: Persist below threshold
checklist.description: While miss_streak is below the configured threshold, presence_buffer.py returns the last observed confidence/state (not zero)

checklist.name: Commit zero above threshold
checklist.description: Once miss_streak reaches or exceeds the threshold, presence_buffer.py returns 0 / no-presence (not the last seen state)

checklist.name: last_conf reset on expiry
checklist.description: When the miss streak expires (reaches threshold), the stored last-seen confidence is reset to 0 so subsequent detection starts fresh

checklist.name: Persistence ~0.8s at 10Hz
checklist.description: The max_misses / threshold value is tuned to approximately 0.8s wall-time at 10fps (i.e., a value of 7–9 misses, not dramatically higher or lower)

checklist.name: Producer-side placement
checklist.description: architecture_notes.md or code comments describe the buffer as sitting on the producer side (between detector output and downstream consumer/actuator input)

checklist.name: Separate actuator debounce layer
checklist.description: architecture_notes.md explicitly states that actuator-side stability filtering / debounce is a SEPARATE layer that should still be kept — the buffer does NOT replace it

checklist.name: Reset streak on detection
checklist.description: When a face IS detected, miss_streak is reset to zero (not accumulated)
